security_privacy_implications: Bind edge execution to explicit client, scope, token, and egress controls; keep secret custody and transport dedupe outside disposable projections.

capability_name: Staff console, client portal, and admin-console surface embodiment
degraded_or_failure_behavior: Rebase, require re-authentication, or fall back to read-only recovery; do not rewrite engine truth to preserve UX continuity.
downstream_phase_implications: Frontend and northbound tasks must model distinct portal, operator, and governance surfaces as consumers of engine-authored semantics.
Route-specific read models should remain replaceable without changing the underlying legal posture vocabulary.
forbidden_shortcuts_or_false_equivalence: re-authoring scope, authority, or workflow semantics per surface
treating portal or admin-console state as command-side truth
using console-local session state to bypass engine truth
lawful_engine_relationship: The broader product may render, secure, route, or transport engine-authored artifacts, but it must not infer or mutate core legal posture locally.
notes: The product includes many surfaces, but none of them becomes the source of manifest, authority, or legal-state truth.
observability_implications: Capture delivery/runtime evidence separately from engine decision evidence and keep projection rebuild paths independent from command-side truth.
security_privacy_implications: Enforce session binding, cache partitioning, least privilege, and secret isolation so delivery layers cannot widen scope or leak protected data.

capability_name: Northbound API gateway, session management, and experience-stream transport
degraded_or_failure_behavior: Rebase, require re-authentication, or fall back to read-only recovery; do not rewrite engine truth to preserve UX continuity.
downstream_phase_implications: API and streaming work must preserve command/read separation, stale-view guards, and duplicate suppression at the edge.
Frontend flows must recover via durable receipts and snapshot rebase rather than by replaying writes heuristically.
forbidden_shortcuts_or_false_equivalence: promoting ExperienceDelta or receipt transport fields into command truth
treating partial POST success as legal state
using reconnect logic to invent missing transitions client-side
lawful_engine_relationship: The broader product may render, secure, route, or transport engine-authored artifacts, but it must not infer or mutate core legal posture locally.
notes: Northbound delivery is required for the product, but the contract itself says command-side truth remains inside the core engine.
observability_implications: Capture delivery/runtime evidence separately from engine decision evidence and keep projection rebuild paths independent from command-side truth.
security_privacy_implications: Enforce session binding, cache partitioning, least privilege, and secret isolation so delivery layers cannot widen scope or leak protected data.

lawful_engine_relationship: The broader product may render, secure, route, or transport engine-authored artifacts, but it must not infer or mutate core legal posture locally.

observability_implications: Capture delivery/runtime evidence separately from engine decision evidence and keep projection rebuild paths independent from command-side truth.

Frontend clients should lean on server-issued session state, not local trust shortcuts, for privileged actions.

lawful_engine_relationship: The broader product may render, secure, route, or transport engine-authored artifacts, but it must not infer or mutate core legal posture locally.

observability_implications: Capture delivery/runtime evidence separately from engine decision evidence and keep projection rebuild paths independent from command-side truth.

capability_name: Identity issuance, HMRC sign-up, and agent authorisation setup
degraded_or_failure_behavior: Remain pending, blocked, or advisory-only until external resolution exists; never assume success from silence or local intent.
downstream_phase_implications: Authority-link and delegation modules must validate imported or existing authority context instead of fabricating it.
User journeys should treat sign-up as an external prerequisite and surface bounded handoff or prerequisite failure posture.
forbidden_shortcuts_or_false_equivalence: implementing HMRC sign-up or primary identity issuance inside the engine
treating local tenant policy as equivalent to authority-issued identity
using RPA or local shortcuts to absorb prohibited sign-up journeys
lawful_engine_relationship: The engine may guide, request, compare, record, or react to this function, but it cannot absorb, impersonate, or overwrite the external actor's authority.
notes: Identity proofing, sign-up, and primary authority issuance remain with HMRC or the relevant external provider.
observability_implications: Record handoff, acknowledgement, comparison, and provenance evidence so external truth and internal intent remain distinguishable.
security_privacy_implications: Treat external state as untrusted until authenticated and correlated; never derive identity issuance or legal truth from local convenience.

forbidden_shortcuts_or_false_equivalence: presenting guided handoff as if the engine completed the task itself
reimplementing HMRC-only journeys inside product flows
using embedded trusted web surfaces to blur the authority boundary
lawful_engine_relationship: The engine may guide, request, compare, record, or react to this function, but it cannot absorb, impersonate, or overwrite the external actor's authority.
notes: The lawful engine relationship is guidance and handoff, not absorption of HMRC-reserved journeys.
observability_implications: Record handoff, acknowledgement, comparison, and provenance evidence so external truth and internal intent remain distinguishable.
security_privacy_implications: Treat external state as untrusted until authenticated and correlated; never derive identity issuance or legal truth from local convenience.

observability_implications: Record handoff, acknowledgement, comparison, and provenance evidence so external truth and internal intent remain distinguishable.

degraded_or_failure_behavior: Remain pending, blocked, or advisory-only until external resolution exists; never assume success from silence or local intent.

lawful_engine_relationship: The engine may guide, request, compare, record, or react to this function, but it cannot absorb, impersonate, or overwrite the external actor's authority.
